Our Story

Born from a love of really good cookies.

Crave Cookies was born out of a simple but powerful idea: everybody deserves a dessert that feels personal. Founded in 2022 by two college friends tired of bland, mass-produced sweets, Crave Cookies started as a small home bakery and quickly grew into a beloved local brand.

Our mission is to create indulgent, handcrafted cookies and milkshakes that bring joy to every bite — made with quality ingredients and a whole lot of heart. Our vision is to become the go-to destination for treat lovers who value flavor, creativity, and aesthetic experience in every order.

Quality
Creativity
Community
Craft
The Team

Meet the people behind the cookies

Jerry Naranjo
Co-Founder & Head Baker

Jerry studied Business Administration at Trent University, where his love for baking and entrepreneurship collided. He co-founded Crave Cookies after turning his late-night dorm room baking sessions into something people genuinely craved. He leads recipe development and quality control, ensuring every batch meets the gold standard.

Pedro Ibarra
Co-Founder & Creative Director

Pedro brings a background in design and social media marketing. He is responsible for the Crave Cookies brand identity, packaging, and the online presence that our fans love and recognize instantly. His creative vision is what gives Crave Cookies its distinct aesthetic and trendy appeal.

Our Journey

From home kitchen to 10,000+ customers

2022
Crave Cookies launches as a home-based bakery with 3 original cookie flavors and a dream.
2023
Opens first physical pop-up location; introduces the signature handcrafted milkshake menu.
2024
Launches online ordering and ships nationwide across Canada. Website goes live.
2025
Reaches 10,000+ satisfied customers and expands menu to 25+ cookie flavors.

Our Menu

Crafted with love.
Impossible to resist.

Two legendary products. Endless combinations. Zero compromises on quality.

Product 01 — Cookies

Signature Cookie Boxes

Our Signature Cookie Boxes are the heart of Crave Cookies. Each box is filled with freshly baked, warm cookies made from scratch using premium ingredients — real butter, high-quality chocolate, and natural flavors.

Brown Butter Choc Chip Salted Caramel Snickerdoodle Double Dark Fudge Strawberry Cheesecake Peanut Butter Bliss
Starting at $12 — Box of 4
  • Made fresh to order — no preservatives
  • Available: Box of 4 · 6 · 12
  • Customizable box combinations
  • Aesthetic gift-ready packaging
Product 02 — Milkshakes

Handcrafted Milkshakes

Our Handcrafted Milkshakes are thick, creamy, and made to pair perfectly with our cookies. Each shake is blended fresh using real ice cream, whole milk, and premium mix-ins — no syrups, no shortcuts.

Classic Vanilla Bean Cookies & Cream Strawberry Dream Salted Caramel Chocolate Overload
Starting at $7 — Regular 16oz
  • Real ice cream base — no syrups
  • Regular (16oz) · Large (24oz)
  • Dairy-free options available
  • Add a cookie pairing for $4 more

Lifestyle & Wellness

5 Reasons Why Treating Yourself to a Cookie Is Actually Good for You

8 min readMarch 2026By Crave Cookies
indulgent treatsself-care dessertsartisan cookiestreating yourself

We live in a world that constantly tells us to optimize, restrict, and deprive ourselves in the name of health. But what if one of the most effective forms of self-care was sitting right there on a baking tray, golden and warm, smelling like brown butter and vanilla?

Treating yourself to an indulgent treat, like a freshly baked artisan cookie, is not the enemy of a balanced lifestyle. In fact, when approached mindfully, it can be a genuinely positive habit. Here are five evidence-backed and psychology-rooted reasons why enjoying a cookie might be exactly what you need.

1. It triggers a genuine mood boost

There is a reason comfort food exists. When you eat something sweet and satisfying, your brain releases dopamine, the neurotransmitter associated with pleasure and reward. This is not just a cliché. Research in the field of nutritional psychology consistently shows that allowing yourself small, intentional indulgences contributes to a positive emotional state and reduces feelings of stress and anxiety.

Unlike mindless snacking, choosing a high-quality, thoughtfully made treat, like a freshly baked artisan cookie made with real ingredients, turns eating into a conscious, pleasurable experience. That mindful moment of enjoyment is, in itself, a form of self-care dessert that nourishes more than just your hunger.

2. Restriction often leads to overconsumption

One of the most well-documented findings in behavioral nutrition is that labeling foods as "forbidden" tends to increase cravings rather than eliminate them. The more we tell ourselves we cannot have something, the more mental energy we spend thinking about it, and the more likely we are to eventually overeat it.

Allowing yourself to enjoy a cookie freely and without guilt, especially one that genuinely tastes amazing, actually supports a healthier long-term relationship with food. Treating yourself in a conscious, portion-controlled way removes the psychological charge from indulgent treats and makes them a normal, enjoyable part of life.

3. Shared treats strengthen social bonds

Food has always been one of humanity's most powerful social connectors. Sharing a box of cookies with a partner, a friend, or a family member is a small but meaningful act of connection. It creates a moment of shared pleasure, opens up conversation, and reinforces the kind of everyday intimacy that keeps relationships strong.

Young couples in particular often find that low-pressure, enjoyable activities, like sitting together with great desserts, are some of the most memorable bonding moments. A box of handcrafted cookies and a milkshake to share is not just a snack. It is an experience.

4. Quality ingredients make a real difference

Not all cookies are created equal. There is a significant difference between a mass-produced, preservative-filled packaged cookie and one made fresh with real butter, high-quality chocolate, and natural flavors. When you choose artisan cookies made from scratch, you are not just choosing better taste, you are choosing a product that was made with care and without the additives and artificial ingredients that come with heavily processed alternatives.

Understanding what goes into your food is an important part of mindful eating. Choosing quality over quantity, one exceptional, freshly baked cookie over a handful of mediocre ones, is a principle that applies just as much to dessert as it does to any other meal.

5. Pleasure is a legitimate human need

Perhaps the most important reason of all: joy matters. Pleasure is not a luxury, it is a legitimate psychological need. A life that has no room for delight, indulgence, or the simple satisfaction of eating something delicious is a life that is missing something important.

The wellness conversation has, for a long time, focused almost entirely on restriction, fewer calories, fewer carbs, fewer treats. But increasingly, experts in mental health and nutrition are pushing back against that narrative and recognizing that true well-being includes the freedom to enjoy food without shame.

So the next time you reach for a cookie and feel a flicker of guilt, remember: treating yourself is not a failure. It is a choice to honor your own happiness. And that, in every sense, is good for you.

The takeaway

Indulgent treats, when chosen with intention and enjoyed mindfully, are not the opposite of a healthy lifestyle, they are part of one. The key is quality, consciousness, and balance. Choose treats that are made well, enjoy them fully, and share them with people you love.

Because life is too short for bad cookies.

Lifestyle & Relationships

How to Plan the Perfect At-Home Date Night (Dessert Included)

9 min readMarch 2026By Crave Cookies
at-home date nightdate night dessertscookie and milkshakeromantic treat ideas

Date nights do not need a reservation, a dress code, or a bill that makes you wince. Some of the most romantic, memorable evenings happen at home, with the right atmosphere, a bit of thoughtfulness, and food that genuinely impresses.

Whether you are in a new relationship or a long-term one, planning a great at-home date night is a skill worth developing. It shows effort, creativity, and care, three things that matter far more than the price tag on a restaurant menu. Here is a step-by-step guide to making your next evening in feel like an event.

Step 1: Set the scene before they arrive

The atmosphere you create before your date even begins sets the tone for the entire evening. Ambiance is everything, and the good news is that it does not require much money to get it right.

  • Lighting: Swap out harsh overhead lights for warm lamps or candles. Soft, warm lighting is one of the single most effective ways to make any space feel intimate and special.
  • Music: Build a playlist in advance. A well-curated playlist signals that you thought ahead. Some lo-fi, jazz, soft R&B, or whatever fits your shared taste.
  • Scent: A candle with a warm, comforting scent; vanilla, sandalwood, or something sweet, adds an often overlooked but powerful sensory dimension to the experience.
  • Tidiness: You do not need a Pinterest-perfect home. Just clear the surfaces, fluff the cushions, and make the space feel intentional.

Step 2: Plan an activity, not just a meal

The most memorable date nights have a structure beyond just eating. An activity gives you something to engage with together, takes the pressure off conversation, and creates shared memories that outlast the evening.

Some ideas that work well at home:

  • A movie marathon with a theme that you both choose in advance
  • A board game or card game with a playful wager involved
  • A cooking challenge where you each make one course
  • A blind tasting of different snacks or desserts, rating them together
  • Building a blanket fort and watching something nostalgic

The activity does not need to be elaborate. What matters is that it is something you do together, not just side by side.

Step 3: Think through the food thoughtfully

Food is one of the most powerful tools you have on a date night, it communicates care and creates shared sensory pleasure. The key is to be intentional rather than default to whatever is in the fridge.

For a full evening, consider a simple structure: something to snack on while you get settled, a main meal that does not require you to spend the whole night in the kitchen, and a dessert that feels special.

The dessert, in particular, deserves real thought. It is the last thing you eat together, and the flavor and experience of it lingers. A well-chosen, high-quality dessert can elevate an entire evening, even if the rest of the meal was simple.

Step 4: Make dessert the highlight

Here is where a lot of people miss an opportunity. Dessert on a date night should not be an afterthought. It should be something you both look forward to, something that feels a little indulgent, a little special, and ideally, something you can share.

The combination of freshly baked cookies and a handcrafted milkshake is one of the most satisfying dessert experiences you can create at home. It is nostalgic, shareable, and deeply satisfying in a way that a store-bought cake simply is not. The contrast of a warm, chewy cookie against a thick, cold milkshake hits every sensory note, texture, temperature, sweetness, and richness, all at once.

For the best experience, think about pairing flavors intentionally. A rich, dark chocolate cookie pairs beautifully with a vanilla bean milkshake. A salted caramel cookie alongside a cookies and cream shake creates a layered, complex flavor profile that feels genuinely gourmet.

Presentation matters too. Serve the cookies warm on a small plate or wooden board, pour the milkshake into a tall glass with a metal straw, and place everything on a tray you bring to wherever you are sitting together. The small effort of plating transforms a snack into a moment.

Step 5: Don't look at your phones

This one is simple but profound. The single biggest upgrade you can make to any date night, at home or otherwise, is to commit to being fully present. Phones face-down, notifications off, for at least the first two hours.

In a world of constant distraction, giving someone your undivided attention is one of the most romantic things you can do. It signals that they are the most interesting thing in your world right now, and that is a feeling that no amount of restaurant ambiance can manufacture.

A final thought

The perfect at-home date night is not about recreating a restaurant experience in your living room. It is about creating something that is uniquely yours, an evening shaped by your shared tastes, your humor, your comfort with each other, and a genuine desire to make the other person feel valued.

Get the atmosphere right, plan something to do together, eat something delicious, and be present. That is really all it takes.

And if the dessert is exceptional? Even better.
